response.justification: Candidate brings 15+ years of polyglot engineering experience, open-source leadership (GISCollective founder), strong Linux/Unix orientation, and exposure to architecture across web, embedded, and microservices — aligning well with the technical and open-source aspects of the role. However, the role demands senior leadership of a team plus German at C1 minimum, and the resume shows only individual-contributor titles (no formal Head/Lead/Manager role with direct reports) and German listed as Basic, which is a hard mismatch with a stated requirement.
strengths: Founder of an open-source GIS platform — demonstrates architecture ownership and OSS alignment
Broad polyglot, Linux-centric background spanning microservices, embedded, and web
Active in mentoring/teaching: workshops, dojos, Selenium course, conference talks
concerns: German listed as Basic — fails the C1 minimum hard requirement
No formal team-lead or Head-of-Development title; leadership experience is implicit, not demonstrated
Recent career gap and short tenure at most recent role raise stability questions for an executive-track hire
